steps.title: Expand the product f(x) * g(x)
steps.content:
To find $b$, we expand $(x^2 + bx)(8x^2 - 40x)$ using the distributive property:
$$\begin{align}(x^2 + bx)(8x^2 - 40x) &= x^2(8x^2 - 40x) + bx(8x^2 - 40x)\\&= 8x^4 - 40x^3 + 8bx^3 - 40bx^2\\&= 8x^4 + (8b - 40)x^3 - 40bx^2\end{align}$$

steps.title: Compare coefficients with the given product
steps.content:
Since $f(x)\cdot g(x)=8x^4-39x^3-5x^2$, the coefficient of $x^3$ in our expansion must equal $-39$:
$$8b-40=-39$$
$$8b=1$$
$$b=\frac{1}{8}$$
That matches Choice C — $\frac{1}{8}$, so C.
